headline: Government responses to parliamentary questions take average 67 days
context: Written parliamentary questions received government responses in an average of 67 days in 2025, with 18% of questions never receiving any response at all.

cards.big: 67 days average to answer a parliamentary question — 18% never answered at all
cards.sub: Government accountability mechanism degraded into bureaucratic dead letter box

cards.big: Standing Order 24 requires 'reasonable time' but defines no specific deadline
cards.sub: Vague requirement allows indefinite delay without technical breach of rules

cards.big: 835 written questions were filed in 2025 — 150 received no response whatsoever
cards.sub: MPs representing millions of citizens had their scrutiny function ignored

cards.big: Australia requires written question responses within 30 sitting days — enforced by Speaker
cards.sub: Clear deadlines with enforcement create functional accountability mechanisms

cards.big: A question that takes 67 days to answer isn't being answered — it's being waited out
Should unanswered parliamentary questions trigger automatic debate on ministerial accountability?
